name: Tomato Rasam
preparation: Soak tamarind in warm water and extract the juice.
Boil tomatoes until soft, mash and strain.
Mix tamarind water, tomato pulp, rasam powder, and dal water in a pot.
Bring to a boil and simmer for 5-7 minutes until frothy.
Heat ghee, splutter mustard seeds, add curry leaves and red chillies.
Pour tempering over the rasam and serve hot.
notes: Serve hot with steamed rice for a comforting meal.

name: Pepper Rasam
preparation: Coarsely crush peppercorns and cumin together.
Extract tamarind juice and mix with dal water.
Crush garlic cloves and add to the tamarind-dal mixture.
Bring to a boil and simmer for 5 minutes.
Heat ghee, splutter mustard seeds, add curry leaves.
Pour tempering into rasam and serve piping hot.
notes: Excellent remedy for cold and sore throat.

name: Garlic Rasam
preparation: Crush garlic, pepper, and cumin to a coarse paste.
Extract tamarind juice and combine with dal water.
Add the garlic-pepper paste to the liquid.
Boil and simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Heat sesame oil, splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Pour tempering into rasam and serve hot.
notes: Known for its immunity-boosting properties.

name: Lemon Rasam
preparation: Cook toor dal with turmeric and mash well.
Mix dal with water and bring to a boil.
Add salt and simmer for 5 minutes.
Heat ghee, splutter mustard seeds, add curry leaves and green chillies.
Pour tempering into the dal mixture.
Remove from heat, add lemon juice and serve immediately.
notes: Add lemon juice after removing from heat to preserve its flavor.

name: Pineapple Rasam
preparation: Cook pineapple pieces with a little water until soft.
Extract tamarind juice and mix with dal water and rasam powder.
Add cooked pineapple to the tamarind mixture.
Bring to a boil and simmer for 5 minutes.
Heat coconut oil, splutter mustard seeds, add curry leaves and red chillies.
Pour tempering into the rasam and serve hot.
notes: Use ripe sweet pineapple for the best flavor balance.

name: Jeera Rasam
preparation: Dry roast cumin seeds and crush coarsely with pepper.
Extract tamarind juice and mix with dal water.
Add crushed cumin-pepper mix and chopped tomato.
Bring to a boil and simmer for 7-8 minutes.
Heat ghee, splutter mustard seeds, add curry leaves.
Pour tempering into rasam and serve hot.
notes: Dry roasting cumin enhances its aroma significantly.

name: Mysore Rasam
preparation: Dry roast coconut, red chillies, and cumin, then grind to a fine paste.
Extract tamarind juice and mix with dal water.
Add the ground coconut paste to the tamarind mixture.
Bring to a boil and simmer for 8-10 minutes.
Heat coconut oil, splutter mustard seeds, add curry leaves.
Pour tempering into the rasam and serve hot.
notes: The coconut paste gives this rasam its characteristic thickness.

name: Milagu Rasam
preparation: Coarsely grind pepper, cumin, and garlic together.
Extract tamarind juice and combine with dal water.
Add the ground pepper-garlic paste to the mixture.
Boil and simmer for 5-7 minutes until aromatic.
Heat sesame oil, splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Pour tempering into rasam and serve immediately.
notes: A traditional Tamil Nadu remedy for cold and congestion.

name: Tamarind Rasam
preparation: Soak tamarind in warm water and extract thick juice.
Mix tamarind juice with dal water and rasam powder.
Add chopped tomatoes and bring to a boil.
Simmer for 8-10 minutes until the raw smell disappears.
Heat ghee, splutter mustard seeds, add curry leaves and red chillies.
Pour tempering into the rasam and garnish with coriander.
notes: The tanginess of tamarind makes this rasam very appetizing.

name: Neem Flower Rasam
preparation: Clean and wash neem flowers thoroughly.
Extract tamarind juice and mix with dal water and jaggery.
Lightly saute neem flowers in a drop of oil.
Add neem flowers to the tamarind mixture and simmer for 5 minutes.
Heat sesame oil, splutter mustard seeds, add curry leaves and chillies.
Pour tempering into the rasam and serve warm.
notes: Traditionally prepared during Ugadi for its medicinal benefits.

name: Tamatar Ka Shorba
preparation: Saute onion, garlic, and ginger in butter until golden.
Add chopped tomatoes and bay leaf, cook until tomatoes are soft.
Remove bay leaf and blend the mixture until smooth.
Strain through a sieve for a silky texture.
Return to heat, add cream, salt, and pepper.
Simmer for 2-3 minutes and serve hot.
notes: A North Indian classic, best served with bread croutons.

name: Palak Shorba
preparation: Blanch spinach in boiling water for 2 minutes, drain and set aside.
Saute onion and garlic in butter until soft.
Add cumin seeds and blanched spinach, cook for 2 minutes.
Blend until smooth and return to the pot.
Add cream, nutmeg, salt, and pepper.
Simmer for 3-4 minutes and serve hot.
notes: Blanching spinach retains its vibrant green color.

name: Dal Shorba
preparation: Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric and water until soft.
Mash the dal well and strain for a smooth consistency.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds, saute onion and ginger until golden.
Add tomato and cook until soft.
Add the strained dal, adjust water, and simmer for 5 minutes.
Squeeze lemon juice, garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A light and protein-rich soup, perfect as a starter.

name: Gosht Shorba Light
preparation: Boil mutton with whole spices, ginger, and onion in 4 cups water.
Skim off any froth that rises to the surface.
Simmer on low heat for 45-60 minutes until meat is tender.
Strain the broth through a fine sieve.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot garnished with fresh coriander leaves.
notes: A restorative broth traditionally served to the sick and elderly.

name: Murgh Shorba Light
preparation: Boil chicken pieces with whole spices, ginger, and onion in 4 cups water.
Skim off any froth and simmer on low for 30-40 minutes.
Strain the broth and shred some chicken meat back in.
Season with salt, pepper, and a squeeze of lemon.
Reheat gently before serving.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.
notes: Use chicken with bones for a richer, more flavorful broth.

name: Mulligatawny Soup
preparation: Saute onion, ginger, and carrot in butter until softened.
Add curry powder and stir for a minute.
Add chicken, lentils, and 3 cups water. Bring to a boil.
Simmer for 25-30 minutes until lentils and chicken are cooked.
Remove chicken, shred and set aside. Blend the soup smooth.
Return chicken to the soup, add coconut milk, and serve hot.
notes: A classic Anglo-Indian soup with South Indian origins.

name: Mulligatawny Veg
preparation: Saute onion, ginger, carrot, and apple in butter.
Add curry powder and stir for a minute.
Add lentils and 3 cups vegetable stock. Bring to a boil.
Simmer for 25 minutes until lentils are soft.
Blend until smooth and return to pot.
Stir in coconut milk, season, and serve hot.
notes: Apple adds a subtle sweetness that balances the curry.

name: Spicy Mulligatawny
preparation: Heat oil, add cumin seeds and saute onion, ginger, green chillies.
Add curry powder and red chilli powder, stir for a minute.
Add lentils and 3 cups water, bring to a boil.
Simmer for 25 minutes until lentils are mushy.
Blend to a smooth consistency.
Add coconut milk, adjust seasoning, and serve hot.
notes: Adjust green chillies for desired spice level.

name: Coconut Mulligatawny
preparation: Heat coconut oil, add cumin seeds and curry leaves.
Saute onion and ginger until golden.
Add lentils, turmeric, and 2 cups water. Boil.
Simmer for 20-25 minutes until lentils are soft.
Blend partially and add coconut milk.
Simmer for 3 minutes and serve garnished with coriander.
notes: The coconut milk makes this version rich and creamy.

name: Lemon Coriander Soup
preparation: Boil vegetable stock with ginger and garlic.
Add corn and cook for 3 minutes.
Chop coriander and add to the stock.
Mix cornflour in water and stir into the soup to thicken.
Season with salt and pepper.
Remove from heat, add lemon juice, and serve hot.
notes: A refreshing and light starter popular in Indian-Chinese cuisine.

name: Lemon Ginger Soup
preparation: Simmer vegetable stock with grated ginger and garlic.
Add diced carrot and turmeric, cook for 5 minutes.
Strain or blend for a smooth texture.
Add honey and season with salt and pepper.
Remove from heat and add lemon juice.
Garnish with coriander and serve warm.
notes: Excellent for soothing sore throat and cold symptoms.

name: Lemon Pepper Soup
preparation: Boil chicken with ginger, garlic, and turmeric until cooked.
Strain the broth and shred chicken.
Add cracked pepper and curry leaves to the broth.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Return shredded chicken to the soup.
Remove from heat, add lemon juice, and serve hot.
notes: Adjust pepper quantity based on your spice tolerance.

name: Mixed Vegetable Soup
ingredients: 1/16 cup each: carrot, beans, peas, corn
preparation: Heat butter, add cumin seeds and saute onion and ginger.
Add all diced vegetables and stir for 2 minutes.
Add 3 cups water and bring to a boil.
Simmer for 15-20 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Blend half the soup and mix back for a semi-thick consistency.
Season with salt and pepper, serve hot.
notes: A wholesome everyday soup packed with nutrients.

name: Clear Sabzi Soup
ingredients: 1/12 cup each: carrot, cabbage, beans julienned
preparation: Bring vegetable stock to a boil with ginger.
Add julienned vegetables and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Season with soy sauce, salt, and pepper.
Vegetables should remain slightly crunchy.
Remove ginger and adjust seasoning.
Serve hot in a bowl.
notes: Keep vegetables slightly crunchy for the best texture.

name: Thick Veg Soup
preparation: Saute onion and garlic in butter until soft.
Add mixed vegetables and stock, boil and simmer for 15 minutes.
Blend half the soup and return to pot.
Mix cornflour in water and add to thicken.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot garnished with fresh herbs.
notes: Cornflour gives this soup its thick, hearty consistency.

name: Tomato Soup
preparation: Saute onion, garlic, and ginger in butter until golden.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until completely soft.
Blend smooth and strain through a sieve.
Return to heat, add sugar, salt, and pepper.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Swirl in cream and serve hot.
notes: Use ripe red tomatoes for the best color and flavor.

name: Roasted Tomato Soup
preparation: Roast tomatoes, garlic, and onion with olive oil at 200C for 25 minutes.
Transfer roasted vegetables to a pot with vegetable stock.
Simmer for 10 minutes.
Blend until smooth and strain if desired.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot garnished with fresh basil.
notes: Roasting caramelizes the tomatoes for a deeper flavor.

name: Tomato Basil
preparation: Saute onion and garlic in olive oil until soft.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook for 10 minutes.
Add basil leaves and cook for another 2 minutes.
Blend smooth and strain.
Season with salt and pepper.
Drizzle cream on top and serve hot.
notes: Add basil at the end to preserve its fresh aroma.

name: Spinach Soup
preparation: Blanch spinach in boiling water for 2 minutes, cool in ice water.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add blanched spinach and stir for a minute.
Blend to a smooth puree with 1 cup water.
Simmer for 5 minutes, add cream and nutmeg.
Season with salt and pepper, serve hot.
notes: Blanching in ice water keeps the spinach bright green.

name: Palak Soup Creamy
preparation: Saute onion and garlic in butter until soft.
Add potato and 2 cups water, cook until potato is tender.
Add spinach and cook for 2 minutes.
Blend until completely smooth.
Stir in cream and nutmeg, adjust seasoning.
Serve hot with a swirl of cream.
notes: Potato adds natural creaminess to the soup.

name: Spinach Moong Soup
preparation: Cook moong dal with turmeric until soft.
Blanch spinach and blend with cooked dal.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and garlic, saute briefly.
Add the spinach-dal puree and ginger.
Simmer for 5-7 minutes, adjust consistency with water.
Add lemon juice and serve hot.
notes: A protein-rich soup combining the goodness of dal and greens.

name: Carrot Soup
preparation: Saute cumin seeds, onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add carrots and 2 cups water, cook until carrots are soft.
Blend until smooth.
Return to heat and simmer for 3-4 minutes.
Add cream, salt, and pepper.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
notes: Carrots give this soup a natural sweetness.

name: Carrot Ginger Indian Soup
preparation: Saute onion, garlic, and ginger in coconut oil.
Add carrots, turmeric, and 2 cups water.
Cook until carrots are very tender.
Blend until smooth.
Stir in coconut milk and season.
Simmer for 2-3 minutes and serve hot.
notes: Extra ginger adds a warming kick to this soup.

name: Carrot Coriander
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add carrots and 2 cups water, cook until tender.
Add coriander leaves and cook for 1 minute.
Blend until smooth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot garnished with coriander sprigs.
notes: Fresh coriander adds a wonderful herbaceous flavor.

name: Beetroot Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add beetroot and 2 cups water, cook until tender.
Blend until smooth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot with a dollop of yogurt.
Squeeze lemon juice on top.
notes: Beetroot gives this soup a stunning deep red color.

name: Beetroot Ginger Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in coconut oil.
Add beetroot and 2 cups water, cook until soft.
Blend until smooth.
Add coconut milk and pepper.
Simmer for 3 minutes.
Serve hot garnished with a swirl of coconut milk.
notes: Ginger complements the earthy sweetness of beetroot.

name: Beetroot Carrot Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add beetroot and carrots with 2 cups water.
Cook until vegetables are tender.
Blend until smooth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot garnished with cream or coriander.
notes: The combination of beetroot and carrot gives a vibrant color.

name: Sweet Corn Shorba
preparation: Saute onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add corn kernels and 2 cups water, cook for 10 minutes.
Blend half the soup and return to pot for texture.
Mix cornflour in water and stir into the soup.
Cook until thickened, season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Use fresh corn for the sweetest flavor.

name: Corn Spinach Soup
preparation: Saute garlic, ginger, and onion in butter.
Add corn and 2 cups water, cook for 8 minutes.
Add spinach and cook for 2 minutes.
Blend partially for a chunky texture.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Spinach adds iron and color to this corn soup.

name: Corn Pepper Soup
preparation: Heat sesame oil, saute garlic, ginger, and spring onion whites.
Add corn and 2 cups stock, cook for 8 minutes.
Mix cornflour in water and stir into the soup.
Add soy sauce and cracked pepper.
Cook until thickened.
Serve hot topped with spring onion greens.
notes: An Indo-Chinese fusion soup with bold pepper flavor.

name: Moong Dal Soup
preparation: Wash and cook moong dal with turmeric until soft.
Mash the dal smooth.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds, saute onion, garlic, and ginger.
Add the mashed dal and 1 cup water.
Simmer for 5 minutes, season with salt.
Add lemon juice and serve hot.
notes: Light and easy to digest, perfect for a light meal.

name: Masoor Dal Soup
preparation: Cook masoor dal with turmeric until completely mushy.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds, saute onion and garlic.
Add tomato and cook until soft.
Add cooked dal, adjust water for soup consistency.
Simmer for 5 minutes, season with salt.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
notes: Masoor dal cooks quickly and doesn't need soaking.

name: Mixed Dal Soup
ingredients: 1/2 tbsp each: toor, moong, masoor dal
preparation: Wash and pressure cook all dals with turmeric.
Mash well until smooth.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds, saute onion and ginger.
Add tomato and cook until soft.
Add mashed dal mixture and water to adjust consistency.
Simmer for 5 minutes, garnish with coriander.
notes: Mixing different dals creates a more complex flavor profile.

name: Toor Dal Soup
preparation: Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric until soft.
Mash the dal and set aside.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and curry leaves.
Saute onion and garlic until golden.
Add tomato, cook until soft, then add mashed dal.
Simmer for 5-7 minutes and serve hot.
notes: Toor dal gives a rich, earthy flavor to this soup.

name: Chana Dal Soup
preparation: Soak chana dal for 30 minutes and pressure cook with turmeric.
Mash the dal until smooth.
Heat ghee, add cumin, saute onion, garlic, and ginger.
Add mashed dal and water to soup consistency.
Simmer for 7-8 minutes, season with salt.
Add lemon juice and serve hot.
notes: Soaking chana dal reduces cooking time significantly.

name: Green Gram Soup
preparation: Soak whole green moong for 2 hours.
Pressure cook with turmeric until very soft.
Mash well or blend partially.
Heat ghee, add cumin, saute onion, garlic, and ginger.
Add the green gram mixture and adjust consistency.
Season with pepper and salt, serve hot.
notes: Whole green moong is highly nutritious and filling.

name: Urad Dal Soup Light
preparation: Soak urad dal for 1 hour and pressure cook with turmeric.
Mash well until smooth.
Heat ghee, add cumin, saute onion, garlic, and ginger.
Add mashed dal and water for soup consistency.
Simmer for 5-7 minutes and season.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
notes: Urad dal is rich in protein and B vitamins.

name: Lauki Ka Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add diced bottle gourd and 2 cups water.
Cook until bottle gourd is very soft.
Blend until smooth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
notes: Very low in calories, ideal for weight management.

name: Lauki Soup
preparation: Cook moong dal and lauki together with turmeric until soft.
Blend until smooth.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and garlic.
Pour tempering into the soup.
Simmer for 3-4 minutes and season.
Serve hot with a squeeze of lemon.
notes: Adding moong dal makes this lauki soup more nutritious.

name: Dudhi Soup
ingredients: 2/3 cups dudhi (bottle gourd) peeled and diced
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add dudhi and 2 cups water, cook until very tender.
Blend until smooth.
Add milk and simmer for 2-3 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Dudhi is the Gujarati/Marathi name for bottle gourd.

name: Mushroom Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add sliced mushrooms and cook until they release moisture.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 10 minutes.
Blend until smooth, return to pot.
Stir in cream, salt, and pepper.
Serve hot garnished with sauteed mushroom slices.
notes: Use a mix of button and cremini mushrooms for depth.

name: Mushroom Pepper Soup
preparation: Saute garlic and onion in butter until soft.
Add mushrooms and cook for 5-6 minutes.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 10 minutes.
Blend smooth and return to heat.
Add cracked pepper and cream.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
notes: Freshly cracked pepper elevates the flavor significantly.

name: Cream Of Mushroom Indian Light
preparation: Saute onion and garlic in butter until translucent.
Add mushrooms and cook until golden.
Add 1.5 cups water and simmer for 8 minutes.
Blend until smooth.
Mix cornflour in milk and add to soup, stir until thickened.
Season with pepper and salt, serve hot.
notes: Uses milk instead of cream for a lighter version.

name: Pumpkin Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add pumpkin, turmeric, and 2 cups water.
Cook until pumpkin is very soft.
Blend until smooth.
Add cream and season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot with toasted pumpkin seeds if desired.
notes: Pumpkin naturally thickens the soup without needing flour.

name: Kaddu Ka Shorba
preparation: Saute onion, garlic, ginger with bay leaf and cinnamon.
Add pumpkin and 2 cups water, cook until soft.
Remove whole spices and blend smooth.
Return to heat and add cream.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot with a swirl of cream.
notes: Whole spices give this shorba a royal Mughlai touch.

name: Pumpkin Ginger
preparation: Saute onion, garlic, and ginger in coconut oil.
Add pumpkin, turmeric, and 2 cups water.
Cook until pumpkin is very tender.
Blend until smooth.
Stir in coconut milk, salt, and pepper.
Simmer for 2 minutes and serve hot.
notes: Ginger and coconut milk pair beautifully with pumpkin.

name: Cauliflower Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add cauliflower and 2 cups water.
Cook until cauliflower is very tender.
Blend until smooth.
Add cream, salt, and pepper.
Serve hot garnished with roasted cumin.
notes: Cauliflower creates a naturally creamy soup texture.

name: Gobi Ka Shorba
preparation: Saute onion and garlic with bay leaf and cloves.
Add cauliflower florets and 2 cups water.
Cook until cauliflower is soft.
Remove whole spices and blend smooth.
Add cream and season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
notes: A delicate shorba perfect as a dinner party starter.

name: Cauliflower Pepper Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, garlic, and onion in butter.
Add cauliflower and 2 cups water, cook until soft.
Blend until smooth.
Add cracked pepper and salt.
Simmer for 3 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
notes: Black pepper adds warmth and aids digestion.

name: Broccoli Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add broccoli florets and 2 cups water.
Cook until broccoli is tender.
Blend until smooth.
Add cream, salt, and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Do not overcook broccoli to retain nutrients and color.

name: Broccoli Almond Soup
preparation: Soak almonds in warm water and peel.
Saute onion and garlic in butter.
Add broccoli and 1.5 cups water, cook until tender.
Add almonds and blend everything smooth.
Add milk and nutmeg, simmer for 3 minutes.
Season with pepper and salt, serve hot.
notes: Almonds add richness and protein without cream.

name: Broccoli Spinach Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger.
Add broccoli and 2 cups water, cook for 8 minutes.
Add spinach and cook for 2 minutes.
Blend until smooth.
Add cream, salt, and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: A power-packed green soup rich in vitamins.

name: Patta Gobi Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger.
Add shredded cabbage and tomato.
Add 2 cups water and cook for 10-12 minutes.
Blend partially for a semi-chunky texture.
Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
notes: Very low calorie soup, great for weight loss diets.

name: Cabbage Pepper Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, garlic, onion, and ginger in butter.
Add cabbage and cook for 3 minutes.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 10 minutes.
Blend smooth and return to pot.
Add cracked pepper and salt.
Serve hot.
notes: Pepper cuts through the mildness of cabbage nicely.

name: Cabbage Clear Soup
preparation: Bring stock to a boil with ginger and garlic.
Add cabbage, carrot, and celery.
Cook for 3-4 minutes keeping vegetables crunchy.
Add soy sauce, salt, and pepper.
Remove ginger pieces.
Serve hot immediately.
notes: A very light, detox-friendly clear soup.

name: Onion Soup
preparation: Slow cook onions in butter until deeply caramelized.
Add garlic, ginger, and cumin seeds.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 15 minutes.
Blend smooth if desired or keep chunky.
Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
notes: Caramelizing onions slowly brings out their natural sweetness.

name: Pyaaz Ka Shorba
preparation: Saute onions in ghee with bay leaf and cinnamon until golden.
Add garlic and cumin seeds, cook for a minute.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove whole spices and blend smooth.
Stir in cream and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: A refined Rajasthani soup with Mughlai influences.

name: Onion Coriander Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, garlic, ginger, and onions in butter until soft.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add coriander leaves and cook for 1 minute.
Blend until smooth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Coriander adds a fresh, herbaceous finish.

name: Methi Soup
preparation: Wash methi leaves thoroughly to remove bitterness.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add methi leaves and cook for 3 minutes.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 10 minutes.
Blend smooth.
Season with salt and pepper, serve hot.
notes: Methi has a slightly bitter taste that is very healthy.

name: Fenugreek Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, and garlic in ghee.
Add potato, turmeric, and 2 cups water.
Cook until potato is tender.
Add fenugreek leaves and cook for 3 minutes.
Blend smooth.
Season and serve hot.
notes: Potato mellows the bitterness of fenugreek.

name: Methi Dal Soup
preparation: Cook moong dal with turmeric until soft.
Wash methi leaves thoroughly.
Heat ghee, add cumin, garlic, and ginger.
Add methi leaves and saute for 2 minutes.
Add cooked dal and water for soup consistency.
Simmer for 5 minutes and serve hot.
notes: Combines the benefits of fenugreek and protein-rich dal.

name: Drumstick Soup
preparation: Pressure cook drumstick pieces with water until soft.
Scoop out the pulp from drumstick shells using a spoon.
Heat ghee, saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger.
Add drumstick pulp and 2 cups water.
Simmer for 5 minutes and season.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
notes: Drumstick is rich in calcium and iron.

name: Moringa Soup
preparation: Wash moringa leaves and pick from stems.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add moringa leaves and turmeric, cook for 3 minutes.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 8 minutes.
Blend smooth.
Season with salt and pepper, serve hot.
notes: Moringa leaves are a superfood packed with nutrients.

name: Drumstick Leaf Soup
preparation: Cook moong dal with turmeric until soft.
Wash drumstick leaves and blanch briefly.
Heat ghee, saute onion, garlic, and ginger.
Add drumstick leaves and cooked dal.
Add water and simmer for 5 minutes.
Blend partially, season, and serve hot.
notes: Dal adds protein to this nutrient-dense leaf soup.

name: Ash Gourd Soup
ingredients: 2/3 cups ash gourd (petha) peeled and diced
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add ash gourd and 2 cups water.
Cook until ash gourd is very soft and translucent.
Blend until smooth.
Add coconut milk and season.
Serve hot.
notes: Ash gourd is cooling and excellent for digestion.

name: Winter Melon Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add winter melon and 2 cups water.
Cook until melon is completely soft.
Blend smooth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Very low calorie and hydrating soup.

name: Petha Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in coconut oil.
Add petha and 2 cups water.
Cook until very tender.
Blend until smooth.
Add coconut milk and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Petha is the Hindi name for ash gourd.

name: Ridge Gourd Soup
ingredients: 2/3 cups ridge gourd (turai) peeled and diced
preparation: Peel ridge gourd and dice.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add ridge gourd, turmeric, and 2 cups water.
Cook until very soft.
Blend smooth.
Season and garnish with coriander.
notes: Ridge gourd is very light and easy to digest.

name: Turai Soup
preparation: Cook moong dal with turmeric until soft.
Saute cumin, onion, and garlic in ghee.
Add turai and cook for 5 minutes.
Add cooked dal and 1 cup water.
Blend partially and simmer for 5 minutes.
Season with pepper and salt, serve hot.
notes: Turai is the Hindi name for ridge gourd.

name: Ridge Gourd Clear Soup
preparation: Bring stock to a boil with ginger and garlic.
Add ridge gourd pieces and cook for 4-5 minutes.
Add soy sauce and pepper.
Season with salt.
Add spring onion greens.
Serve hot immediately.
notes: A very light Indo-Chinese style clear soup.

name: Ivy Gourd Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add ivy gourd and turmeric, cook for 3 minutes.
Add 2 cups water and simmer until tender.
Blend smooth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Ivy gourd is known to help regulate blood sugar levels.

name: Tindora Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add tindora and cook for 5 minutes.
Add 2 cups water and simmer until soft.
Blend until smooth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Tindora is the Gujarati name for ivy gourd.

name: Kundru Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add kundru and turmeric, stir for 2 minutes.
Add 2 cups water and cook until tender.
Blend smooth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Kundru is the Hindi name for ivy gourd.

name: Raw Banana Soup
preparation: Peel raw bananas and soak in water to prevent browning.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add raw banana and turmeric, cook for 3 minutes.
Add 2 cups water and cook until very soft.
Blend smooth.
Season with salt and pepper, serve hot.
notes: Raw banana adds a starchy thickness to the soup.

name: Plantain Soup
preparation: Saute onion, garlic, ginger, and curry leaves in coconut oil.
Add plantain and turmeric with 2 cups water.
Cook until plantain is very tender.
Blend until smooth.
Add coconut milk and season.
Serve hot.
notes: Coconut milk adds a South Indian touch to this soup.

name: Kachhe Kele Ka Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add raw banana pieces and 2 cups water.
Cook until bananas are very soft.
Blend smooth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
notes: Kachhe kele means raw banana in Hindi.

name: Kokum Soup
preparation: Soak kokum in warm water for 15 minutes.
Extract kokum juice and strain.
Heat a pan, add cumin seeds, curry leaves, garlic, and chillies.
Add kokum extract, coconut milk, and 1 cup water.
Add salt and sugar, bring to a gentle simmer.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
notes: A Konkan specialty known for its digestive properties.

name: Sol Kadhi
preparation: Soak kokum in 1 cup warm water for 20 minutes.
Grind garlic, green chillies, and cumin to a paste.
Add the paste to coconut milk and mix well.
Add kokum extract and water, mix thoroughly.
Add salt and curry leaves.
Chill and serve cold or at room temperature.
notes: Traditionally served chilled as a digestive after meals.

name: Kokum Rasam
preparation: Soak kokum and tamarind separately in warm water.
Extract juices and mix with dal water and turmeric.
Bring to a boil and simmer for 5 minutes.
Heat coconut oil, splutter mustard seeds.
Add curry leaves and red chillies.
Pour tempering into rasam and serve hot.
notes: Kokum and tamarind together create a unique sour flavor.

name: Amla Soup
preparation: Boil amla in water until soft.
Remove seeds and blend the flesh smooth.
Add ginger, turmeric, cumin, and pepper.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add honey or jaggery to balance sourness.
Serve warm.
notes: Extremely rich in Vitamin C and immunity boosting.

name: Gooseberry Soup
preparation: Cook amla with ginger and turmeric in water until soft.
Blend smooth and strain.
Dry roast cumin and crush, add to soup.
Add pepper, honey, and salt.
Simmer for 2-3 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve warm.
notes: Best consumed during winter for immunity.

name: Amla Pepper Soup
preparation: Boil amla until soft, deseed, and blend.
Add ginger, turmeric, and 2 cups water.
Bring to a simmer.
Add cracked pepper, cumin, and jaggery.
Add curry leaves and simmer for 5 minutes.
Serve warm.
notes: Pepper enhances the absorption of Vitamin C from amla.

name: Ginger Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, and garlic in ghee.
Add grated ginger and turmeric.
Add 2 cups water and bring to a boil.
Simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain or blend, add pepper and salt.
Add lemon juice and serve hot.
notes: A potent remedy for cold, cough, and sore throat.

name: Ginger Turmeric Soup
preparation: Simmer ginger, garlic, and onion in water for 10 minutes.
Add turmeric and continue simmering for 5 minutes.
Strain the broth.
Add pepper, salt, and honey.
Add lemon juice off the heat.
Serve warm.
notes: Anti-inflammatory and immunity-boosting combination.

name: Ginger Garlic Soup
preparation: Crush garlic and ginger to a paste.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and the paste.
Saute for a minute until fragrant.
Add turmeric and 2 cups water.
Simmer for 10 minutes, strain.
Add pepper, lemon juice, and coriander.
notes: Double the garlic and ginger if fighting a cold.

name: Turmeric Milk Soup
preparation: Warm milk with ginger and cinnamon on low heat.
Add turmeric and stir well.
Simmer for 5 minutes, do not boil.
Add pepper and nutmeg.
Remove cinnamon, add ghee and honey.
Serve warm.
notes: Also known as golden milk, a traditional Ayurvedic remedy.

name: Haldi Ka Shorba
preparation: Saute cumin, bay leaf, onion, garlic, and ginger.
Add turmeric and 2 cups water.
Simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove bay leaf and blend smooth.
Add cream and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: A warming North Indian turmeric soup.

name: Golden Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, garlic, and ginger in coconut oil.
Add carrots, turmeric, and 2 cups water.
Cook until carrots are soft.
Blend smooth.
Add coconut milk and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Carrots and turmeric create a beautiful golden color.

name: Neem Soup Light
preparation: Wash neem leaves thoroughly.
Boil neem leaves in 2 cups water for 5 minutes.
Add garlic, tamarind extract, and jaggery.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Heat ghee, splutter cumin and curry leaves.
Pour tempering into the soup and serve warm.
notes: Bitter but highly medicinal, traditionally used as a blood purifier.

name: Bitter Gourd Soup
preparation: Slice karela thin and salt for 10 minutes to reduce bitterness.
Rinse and squeeze out excess water.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add karela and turmeric, cook for 5 minutes.
Add 2 cups water and jaggery, simmer for 10 minutes.
Blend smooth, season, and serve warm.
notes: Excellent for diabetics and blood sugar management.

name: Karela Soup
preparation: Salt karela slices for 15 minutes, rinse and squeeze.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in sesame oil.
Add karela and turmeric, cook for 5 minutes.
Add 2 cups water and jaggery, simmer for 10 minutes.
Blend until smooth.
Season with salt and serve warm.
notes: Jaggery helps balance the bitterness of karela.

name: Snake Gourd Soup
preparation: Peel and dice snake gourd, remove seeds if mature.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add snake gourd and turmeric with 2 cups water.
Cook until very tender.
Blend smooth and season.
Serve hot.
notes: Use young snake gourd for the best flavor.

name: Padwal Soup
preparation: Cook moong dal with turmeric until soft.
Saute cumin, onion, and garlic in ghee.
Add padwal and cook for 5 minutes.
Add cooked dal and 1 cup water.
Blend smooth and simmer for 3 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Padwal is the Marathi name for snake gourd.

name: Chichinda Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add chichinda and turmeric.
Add 2 cups water and simmer until soft.
Blend smooth.
Add pepper and salt.
Serve hot.
notes: Chichinda is light and easy to digest.

name: Jackfruit Soup
preparation: Boil raw jackfruit pieces until tender.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in coconut oil.
Add cooked jackfruit and turmeric.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 10 minutes.
Blend smooth, add coconut milk.
Season and serve hot.
notes: Raw jackfruit has a meaty texture when cooked.

name: Raw Jackfruit Soup
preparation: Boil raw jackfruit until fork-tender.
Saute onion, garlic, ginger, and curry leaves in coconut oil.
Add jackfruit and turmeric.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 8 minutes.
Blend smooth and season.
Serve hot garnished with curry leaves.
notes: Apply oil on hands before cutting raw jackfruit to avoid stickiness.

name: Kathal Ka Soup
preparation: Pressure cook jackfruit until soft.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add cooked jackfruit and garam masala.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 5 minutes.
Blend smooth.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Kathal is the Hindi name for jackfruit.

name: Murgh Shorba
preparation: Boil chicken with onion, garlic, ginger, bay leaf, and turmeric.
Skim froth and simmer for 30 minutes.
Remove chicken, shred meat, discard bones.
Strain broth and return shredded chicken.
Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
notes: Use chicken with bones for a richer, more flavorful broth.

name: Murgh Yakhni
preparation: Marinate chicken in yogurt for 15 minutes.
Boil chicken with all whole spices, onion, and ginger.
Simmer on low heat for 35-40 minutes.
Strain the clear broth.
Season with salt.
Serve hot garnished with saffron strands if available.
notes: A traditional Kashmiri clear broth, very aromatic and light.

name: Chicken Clear Soup
preparation: Boil chicken in 3 cups water with ginger and garlic.
Remove chicken, shred into thin strips.
Strain broth and bring to a simmer.
Add carrot, celery, and shredded chicken.
Add soy sauce and pepper.
Top with spring onions and serve hot.
notes: A light and comforting Indo-Chinese style soup.

name: Chicken Pepper Soup
preparation: Boil chicken with ginger, garlic, and turmeric.
Cook until chicken is tender, shred the meat.
Strain broth and return chicken.
Heat ghee, add cumin, curry leaves, and cracked pepper.
Pour tempering into the soup.
Serve hot.
notes: A South Indian style pepper chicken broth.

name: Chicken Lemon Soup
preparation: Boil chicken with ginger, garlic, and turmeric.
Simmer until chicken is cooked through.
Shred chicken and return to broth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Remove from heat, add lemon juice.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Add lemon juice after removing from heat to preserve flavor.

name: Chicken Ginger Soup
preparation: Saute onion, garlic, and ginger in sesame oil.
Add chicken and turmeric, cook for 3 minutes.
Add 3 cups water and bring to a boil.
Simmer for 25 minutes until chicken is tender.
Shred chicken, season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
notes: Extra ginger makes this soup great for fighting colds.

name: Mutton Broth
preparation: Boil mutton with all spices, onion, garlic, and ginger.
Skim froth and simmer for 60 minutes on low heat.
Cook until mutton is very tender.
Strain the broth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot garnished with coriander.
notes: Slow cooking extracts maximum flavor from the bones.

name: Mutton Yakhni
preparation: Marinate mutton in yogurt for 30 minutes.
Boil mutton with whole spices, onion, and ginger.
Simmer on very low heat for 1 hour.
Strain the clear golden broth.
Add saffron strands.
Season with salt and serve hot.
notes: A Kashmiri delicacy, often served during weddings.

name: Paya Soup Light
preparation: Clean trotters thoroughly and pressure cook with all spices.
Cook for 45-60 minutes until gelatinous.
Strain the thick broth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Reheat gently before serving.
Garnish with coriander and ginger juliennes.
notes: Rich in collagen, traditionally considered good for joints.

name: Fish Soup
preparation: Saute onion, garlic, ginger, and curry leaves in coconut oil.
Add turmeric and 2 cups water, bring to a boil.
Add fish cubes and simmer for 8-10 minutes.
Add coconut milk gently.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot with lemon wedge.
notes: Use firm fish like seer or pomfret that won't break apart.

name: Malabar Fish Soup
preparation: Saute curry leaves, ginger, garlic, and green chillies.
Add kokum soaked water and 2 cups water.
Bring to a boil and add fish pieces.
Simmer for 8 minutes until fish is cooked.
Add coconut milk and season.
Serve hot.
notes: Kokum gives this Malabar soup its signature tangy flavor.

name: Goan Fish Soup Light
preparation: Extract tamarind juice.
Saute onion, garlic, and ginger in coconut oil.
Add recheado masala and tamarind juice.
Add 2 cups water and bring to a simmer.
Add fish and cook for 10 minutes.
Add coconut milk, season, and serve.
notes: A tangy and spicy Goan-style fish broth.

name: Prawn Soup
preparation: Saute onion, garlic, ginger, and curry leaves in coconut oil.
Add turmeric and 2 cups water.
Bring to a boil, add prawns.
Cook for 5-6 minutes until prawns turn pink.
Add coconut milk and season.
Serve hot.
notes: Do not overcook prawns or they become rubbery.

name: Shrimp Rasam
preparation: Extract tamarind juice and mix with dal water.
Add pepper and bring to a boil.
Add shrimp and cook for 4-5 minutes.
Heat sesame oil, splutter mustard seeds.
Add curry leaves and red chillies.
Pour tempering into rasam and serve.
notes: A coastal variation of traditional rasam.

name: Prawn Clear Soup
preparation: Bring stock to a boil with ginger and garlic.
Add prawns and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add celery and spring onion whites.
Season with soy sauce and pepper.
Cook for 2 more minutes.
Top with spring onion greens and serve.
notes: Keep prawns slightly undercooked as they continue cooking in hot broth.

name: Egg Drop Soup
preparation: Bring stock to a boil with ginger, garlic, and turmeric.
Reduce to a gentle simmer.
Slowly drizzle beaten eggs while stirring in one direction.
Egg will form silky ribbons.
Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Pour eggs slowly in a thin stream for delicate ribbons.

name: Egg Lemon Soup
preparation: Cook rice in stock with ginger and garlic until soft.
Beat eggs with lemon juice.
Temper the egg mixture with a ladle of hot stock.
Slowly pour egg mixture into the soup while stirring.
Cook on low heat for 2 minutes, do not boil.
Season and serve immediately.
notes: Do not boil after adding eggs or they will curdle.

name: Egg Pepper Soup
preparation: Hard boil eggs, peel and halve.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add 2 cups water and cracked pepper.
Simmer for 8 minutes.
Blend the broth smooth, place eggs in bowls.
Pour hot soup over eggs and serve.
notes: A South Indian style pepper soup with boiled eggs.

name: Sambhar Soup
preparation: Cook toor dal until soft and mash.
Extract tamarind juice.
Boil drumstick in tamarind water with sambar powder.
Add dal, onion, and tomato, simmer for 10 minutes.
Heat oil, splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Pour tempering into the sambhar soup and serve.
notes: A South Indian staple served as soup or with rice/idli.

name: Rasam Broth
preparation: Extract tamarind juice and mix with dal water.
Add chopped tomato and bring to a boil.
Crush pepper and cumin, add to the broth.
Simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Heat ghee, splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Pour tempering into rasam and serve hot.
notes: A basic rasam template that can be customized with various spices.

name: Kuzhambu Soup
preparation: Cook toor dal until soft.
Extract tamarind juice and mix with sambar powder.
Boil vegetables in tamarind water until tender.
Add cooked dal and simmer for 5 minutes.
Heat sesame oil, splutter mustard seeds, fenugreek, and curry leaves.
Pour tempering and serve.
notes: Kuzhambu is a Tamil style thick tamarind-based soup.

name: Coconut Milk Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, curry leaves, ginger, and garlic in coconut oil.
Add vegetables, turmeric, and 1 cup water.
Cook until vegetables are tender.
Add coconut milk and bring to a gentle simmer.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Do not boil vigorously after adding coconut milk.

name: Thengai Soup
preparation: Grind coconut with a little water to extract thick milk.
Saute cumin, curry leaves, onion, garlic, and ginger.
Add 2 cups water and bring to a boil.
Add coconut milk and simmer gently.
Add pepper and salt.
Serve hot.
notes: Thengai means coconut in Tamil.

name: Coconut Rasam
preparation: Grind coconut with cumin and red chillies to a paste.
Extract tamarind juice and mix with dal water.
Add coconut paste and bring to a boil.
Simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Heat coconut oil, splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Pour tempering into rasam and serve.
notes: Similar to Mysore rasam with a rich coconut flavor.

name: Curry Leaf Soup
preparation: Wash curry leaves and saute in ghee with garlic.
Add cumin and pepper.
Extract tamarind juice, add with dal water.
Simmer for 8 minutes.
Blend smooth and strain.
Serve hot.
notes: Curry leaves are rich in iron and excellent for hair health.

name: Curry Leaf Rasam
preparation: Extract tamarind juice and mix with dal water.
Add pepper and bring to a boil.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Heat sesame oil, splutter mustard seeds.
Add curry leaves and red chillies.
Pour tempering into rasam and serve.
notes: Use very fresh curry leaves for the best aroma.

name: Karivepaku Soup
preparation: Saute curry leaves and garlic in sesame oil.
Add cumin, pepper, and turmeric.
Extract tamarind juice, add with 2 cups water.
Simmer for 8-10 minutes.
Blend and strain.
Serve hot.
notes: Karivepaku is the Telugu name for curry leaves.

name: Mint Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add mint leaves and cook for 1 minute.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 5 minutes.
Blend smooth.
Add yogurt, salt, and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Mint soup is cooling and aids digestion.

name: Pudina Shorba
preparation: Saute cumin, bay leaf, onion, and garlic in butter.
Add mint leaves and stir briefly.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove bay leaf and blend smooth.
Add cream and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Pudina shorba is a refreshing Mughlai-style starter.

name: Mint Pepper Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add mint leaves and cook briefly.
Add 2 cups water and cracked pepper.
Simmer for 5 minutes and blend.
Add lemon juice and salt.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A refreshing and warming soup with bold pepper flavor.

name: Coriander Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add coriander leaves and stir for a minute.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 5 minutes.
Blend smooth.
Add cream and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Use stems along with leaves for extra flavor.

name: Dhaniya Shorba
preparation: Saute cumin, bay leaf, onion, and garlic in ghee.
Add coriander leaves and cook briefly.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove bay leaf and blend smooth.
Add cream, salt, and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Dhaniya is the Hindi name for coriander.

name: Coriander Lemon Soup
preparation: Simmer stock with ginger and garlic for 5 minutes.
Add coriander and cumin, cook for 2 minutes.
Blend smooth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Remove from heat, add lemon juice.
Serve hot.
notes: A light and tangy soup perfect as a starter.

name: Tomato Pepper Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add tomatoes and cook until completely soft.
Blend smooth and strain.
Add cracked pepper and salt.
Simmer for 3 minutes.
Serve hot.
notes: Pepper and tomato make a classic flavor combination.

name: Tomato Cumin Soup
preparation: Dry roast cumin until aromatic.
Saute onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add tomatoes and turmeric, cook until soft.
Add roasted cumin and blend smooth.
Strain, season, and simmer for 3 minutes.
Garnish with coriander.
notes: Dry roasting cumin intensifies its earthy flavor.

name: Tomato Ginger Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and extra ginger in butter.
Add tomatoes and cook until soft.
Blend smooth and strain.
Season with salt and pepper.
Simmer for 3-4 minutes.
Garnish with coriander.
notes: Extra ginger gives a warming kick to classic tomato soup.

name: Masala Corn Soup
preparation: Saute onion, garlic, ginger, and green chillies in butter.
Add corn and 2 cups water, cook for 10 minutes.
Blend half, return to pot for texture.
Add garam masala, salt, and pepper.
Simmer for 3 minutes.
Serve hot.
notes: Garam masala gives a warm Indian twist to corn soup.

name: Baby Corn Soup
preparation: Saute onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add baby corn and 2 cups stock, cook for 8 minutes.
Blend partially for texture.
Mix cornflour in water and add to thicken.
Add soy sauce and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Baby corn adds a delicate crunch to this soup.

name: Corn Coriander Soup
preparation: Saute garlic, ginger, and onion in butter.
Add corn and 2 cups water, cook for 8 minutes.
Add coriander leaves and cook for 1 minute.
Blend smooth.
Season with pepper and salt.
Serve hot.
notes: Coriander adds a fresh herbaceous note to sweet corn.

name: Millet Soup
preparation: Wash millets and soak for 30 minutes.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add millets, turmeric, and 3 cups water.
Cook until millets are very soft.
Blend partially for texture.
Season and serve hot.
notes: Millets add fiber and make this soup very filling.

name: Ragi Soup
preparation: Mix ragi flour in cold water to prevent lumps.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add ragi mixture and 2 cups water.
Stir continuously and cook for 8-10 minutes.
Add buttermilk and pepper.
Season and serve warm.
notes: Ragi is rich in calcium and excellent for bones.

name: Bajra Soup
preparation: Mix bajra flour in cold water to a smooth paste.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add bajra paste, turmeric, and 2 cups water.
Stir well and cook for 10 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Bajra is warming and perfect for winter soups.

name: Jowar Soup
preparation: Mix jowar flour in cold water to prevent lumps.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add jowar mixture and 2 cups water.
Cook stirring for 8-10 minutes until thickened.
Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with coriander.
notes: Jowar is gluten-free and rich in fiber.

name: Foxtail Millet Soup
preparation: Wash and soak foxtail millet for 20 minutes.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add millet, vegetables, turmeric, and 3 cups water.
Cook until millet is very soft.
Blend partially.
Season and serve hot.
notes: Foxtail millet is gluten-free and easily digestible.

name: Little Millet Soup
preparation: Wash and soak little millet for 20 minutes.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add millet, vegetables, and 3 cups water.
Cook until millet is soft.
Blend partially for a semi-thick texture.
Season with pepper and salt.
notes: Little millet has a mild flavor that blends well in soups.

name: Kollu Soup
preparation: Soak horse gram overnight and pressure cook until soft.
Mash and strain to extract thick water.
Heat sesame oil, add cumin, garlic, and curry leaves.
Add horse gram extract and tamarind juice.
Add pepper and simmer for 5 minutes.
Season and serve hot.
notes: Horse gram is a powerhouse of protein, popular in South India.

name: Horse Gram Soup
preparation: Soak horse gram overnight and pressure cook with turmeric.
Mash well or blend partially.
Heat ghee, add cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger.
Add horse gram mixture and water for consistency.
Add pepper and salt, simmer for 5 minutes.
Serve hot.
notes: Known to aid weight loss and is very warming.

name: Horse Gram Rasam
preparation: Soak and boil horse gram until soft, strain the water.
Extract tamarind juice.
Mix horse gram water, tamarind juice, pepper, and cumin.
Bring to a boil and simmer for 5 minutes.
Heat sesame oil, splutter mustard seeds, add garlic and curry leaves.
Pour tempering into rasam and serve.
notes: A traditional Tamil Nadu rasam with medicinal properties.

name: Sattu Soup
preparation: Mix sattu in cold water to a smooth paste.
Heat mustard oil, add cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger.
Add sattu mixture and 2 cups water.
Stir well and cook for 8 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper.
Add lemon juice and serve.
notes: Sattu is a traditional Bihar superfood rich in protein.

name: Sattu Shorba
preparation: Mix sattu in cold water smoothly.
Heat ghee, add cumin, bay leaf, onion, and garlic.
Add sattu mixture and 2 cups water.
Stir and cook for 8-10 minutes.
Remove bay leaf, add pepper and salt.
Garnish with coriander.
notes: A protein-rich shorba from Eastern India.

name: Sattu Clear Soup
preparation: Dissolve sattu in 2 cups water, strain for a clear liquid.
Bring to a simmer with ginger and garlic.
Add cumin and pepper.
Cook for 5 minutes.
Remove from heat, add lemon juice.
Garnish with coriander.
notes: A lighter version of sattu soup, very refreshing.

name: Mung Bean Sprout Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add sprouts and turmeric, cook for 3 minutes.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 10 minutes.
Blend partially.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Sprouting increases the nutritional value of mung beans.

name: Sprout Clear Soup
preparation: Bring stock to a boil with ginger and garlic.
Add sprouts and celery.
Cook for 5 minutes.
Add soy sauce and pepper.
Top with spring onion greens.
Serve hot.
notes: Keep sprouts slightly crunchy for best texture.

name: Sprout Shorba
preparation: Saute cumin, bay leaf, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add sprouts and 2 cups water.
Cook until sprouts are tender.
Remove bay leaf and blend smooth.
Add cream and season.
Serve hot.
notes: Sprout shorba is rich in plant-based protein.

name: Paneer Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger.
Add 2 cups water and simmer for 5 minutes.
Blend smooth and return to pot.
Add paneer cubes, cream, and pepper.
Simmer for 3 minutes.
Garnish with coriander.
notes: Add paneer at the end to keep it soft.

name: Paneer Pepper Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add 2 cups water and simmer.
Blend the base smooth.
Add paneer cubes, cream, and cracked pepper.
Simmer for 3-4 minutes.
Serve hot.
notes: Freshly cracked pepper pairs wonderfully with paneer.

name: Paneer Tomato Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger.
Add tomatoes and cook until soft.
Blend smooth and strain.
Return to heat, add cream and pepper.
Add paneer cubes and simmer for 3 minutes.
Serve hot.
notes: Paneer adds protein to this classic tomato soup.

name: Tofu Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger.
Add turmeric and 2 cups water.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add tofu cubes and soy sauce.
Cook for 3-4 minutes.
Season with pepper and serve.
notes: Tofu is a great vegan protein source for soups.

name: Soya Soup
preparation: Soak soya granules in hot water for 10 minutes, squeeze dry.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add tomato and turmeric, cook until soft.
Add soya granules and 2 cups water.
Simmer for 10 minutes.
Blend partially, season, and serve.
notes: Soya granules are an affordable high-protein ingredient.

name: Soya Chunk Soup
preparation: Soak soya chunks in hot water for 15 minutes, squeeze dry.
Saute onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add tomato and cook until soft.
Add soya chunks, garam masala, and 2 cups water.
Simmer for 10 minutes.
Season with pepper and serve.
notes: Squeeze soya chunks well after soaking to remove excess water.

name: Peanut Soup
preparation: Grind roasted peanuts to a smooth paste with water.
Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in coconut oil.
Add peanut paste, turmeric, and 2 cups water.
Simmer for 10 minutes stirring occasionally.
Add coconut milk and season.
Serve hot.
notes: Peanut soup is rich and creamy without dairy.

name: Groundnut Soup
preparation: Roast and grind groundnuts to a fine paste.
Saute cumin, onion, and garlic in ghee.
Add tomato and cook until soft.
Add groundnut paste, turmeric, and 2 cups water.
Simmer for 10 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A protein-rich soup with a nutty flavor.

name: Peanut Tomato Soup
preparation: Roast peanuts and grind to a paste.
Saute cumin, onion, and garlic in ghee.
Add tomatoes and cook until mushy.
Blend smooth with peanut paste.
Add chilli powder and 1 cup water, simmer for 5 minutes.
Garnish with coriander.
notes: Peanut and tomato create a rich, tangy flavor.

name: Tamarind Soup
preparation: Soak tamarind and extract thick juice.
Saute cumin, curry leaves, onion, garlic, and ginger.
Add tamarind juice, jaggery, and 2 cups water.
Simmer for 8-10 minutes.
Season with salt.
Serve warm.
notes: Jaggery balances the sourness of tamarind perfectly.

name: Pulusu
preparation: Extract thick tamarind juice.
Heat sesame oil, splutter mustard seeds.
Add curry leaves, red chillies, onion, and garlic.
Add tamarind juice, turmeric, and 2 cups water.
Simmer for 10 minutes.
Season and serve hot with rice.
notes: Pulusu is a tangy Andhra dish, similar to rasam but thicker.

name: Charu
preparation: Extract tamarind juice and mix with dal water.
Add tomato, pepper, and cumin.
Bring to a boil and simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Heat ghee, splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Pour tempering into the charu.
Serve hot.
notes: Charu is the Telugu name for rasam.

name: Mixed Sprout Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in ghee.
Add sprouts, tomato, and turmeric.
Add 2 cups water and cook until sprouts are tender.
Blend partially for texture.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Using multiple sprouts creates a richer nutritional profile.

name: Matar Soup
preparation: Saute cumin, onion, garlic, and ginger in butter.
Add peas and 2 cups water, cook until soft.
Add mint leaves and blend smooth.
Return to heat, add cream.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Fresh peas make a sweeter, more vibrant soup.

name: Minestrone
preparation: Saute onion and garlic in olive oil.
Add tomato and cook until soft.
Add vegetables, beans, and 3 cups water.
Simmer for 15 minutes.
Add pasta and cook until al dente.
Season and garnish with basil.
notes: A hearty Italian vegetable soup that is very filling.

name: Classic Minestrone
preparation: Saute onion, celery, and carrot in olive oil.
Add canned tomatoes and 3 cups water.
Add zucchini and beans, simmer for 15 minutes.
Add pasta and cook until done.
Season with salt, pepper, and herbs.
Serve hot with parmesan if desired.
notes: A classic Italian recipe that varies by season.

name: Garden Minestrone
preparation: Saute onion and garlic in olive oil.
Add tomatoes and 3 cups water.
Add seasonal vegetables and beans.
Simmer for 15-20 minutes until tender.
Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with fresh basil.
notes: Use whatever fresh vegetables are in season.

name: Tom Yum
preparation: Bruise lemongrass and add to boiling stock with galangal and lime leaves.
Simmer for 5 minutes to infuse flavors.
Add mushrooms and tomato.
Cook for 3 minutes.
Add chillies, lime juice, and season.
Serve hot.
notes: The aromatic herbs are for flavoring only, not meant to be eaten.

name: Tom Yum Goong
preparation: Boil 3 cups water with lemongrass, galangal, and lime leaves.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add mushrooms and cook for 2 minutes.
Add prawns and cook until pink.
Add fish sauce, lime juice, and chillies.
Serve immediately.
notes: Tom Yum Goong is Thailand's famous hot and sour prawn soup.

name: Tom Kha Gai
preparation: Simmer coconut milk with lemongrass, galangal, and lime leaves.
Add chicken and cook for 8-10 minutes.
Add fish sauce and chillies.
Remove from heat, add lime juice.
Adjust seasoning.
Serve hot.
notes: A creamy Thai coconut chicken soup with aromatic herbs.

name: Miso Soup
preparation: Prepare dashi stock with kombu.
Add mushrooms and rehydrated wakame.
Simmer for 3 minutes.
Remove from heat, dissolve miso paste in a ladle of broth.
Add tofu cubes gently.
Top with spring onions and serve.
notes: Never boil miso soup after adding miso paste.

name: Tofu Miso Soup
preparation: Prepare dashi and bring to a simmer.
Add mushrooms and rehydrated wakame.
Cook for 3 minutes.
Add tofu cubes.
Remove from heat, dissolve miso paste.
Add soy sauce, top with spring onions.
notes: Extra tofu makes this version more protein-rich.

name: Wakame Miso Soup
preparation: Rehydrate wakame in water and cut into pieces.
Prepare dashi and bring to a simmer.
Add wakame and cook for 2 minutes.
Add tofu cubes.
Remove from heat, dissolve miso paste.
Top with spring onions and sesame seeds.
notes: Wakame is rich in iodine and minerals.

name: French Onion Soup Light
preparation: Slow cook onions in butter for 25-30 minutes until deeply caramelized.
Add garlic, thyme, and bay leaf.
Add stock and simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove bay leaf and thyme.
Ladle into bowls, top with toasted bread and cheese.
Broil until cheese melts.
notes: Patience in caramelizing onions is the key to great flavor.

name: Provencal Onion Soup
preparation: Saute onions in olive oil until golden.
Add garlic and tomatoes.
Add herbs de Provence and thyme.
Add stock and simmer for 20 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: A lighter, Mediterranean take on onion soup.

name: Caramelized Onion Soup
preparation: Melt butter and slowly caramelize onions for 30 minutes.
Add garlic and thyme, cook for 2 minutes.
Add bay leaf and stock.
Simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove bay leaf and thyme.
Season and serve hot.
notes: Low and slow caramelization creates deep, sweet flavor.

name: Gazpacho
preparation: Roughly chop all vegetables.
Soak bread in water briefly.
Blend all ingredients until smooth.
Add olive oil and vinegar.
Season with salt and pepper.
Chill for at least 1 hour and serve cold.
notes: A refreshing Spanish cold soup, perfect for hot weather.

name: Watermelon Gazpacho
preparation: Blend watermelon, tomatoes, cucumber, and garlic.
Add olive oil and lime juice.
Season with salt.
Chill for 1 hour.
Serve cold garnished with mint.
Drizzle olive oil on top.
notes: Watermelon adds natural sweetness and hydration.

name: Green Gazpacho
preparation: Roughly chop all vegetables.
Blend until smooth with herbs.
Add olive oil and lime juice.
Season with salt and pepper.
Chill for 1 hour.
Serve cold.
notes: Avocado makes this gazpacho extra creamy.

name: Lentil Soup Mediterranean
preparation: Saute onion, garlic, and carrot in olive oil.
Add lentils, tomatoes, cumin, and 3 cups water.
Bring to a boil and simmer for 25-30 minutes.
Lentils should be tender.
Season with salt and pepper.
Add lemon juice and serve.
notes: A wholesome Mediterranean staple, rich in fiber and protein.

name: Red Lentil Soup
preparation: Saute onion, garlic, and carrot in olive oil.
Add lentils, cumin, turmeric, and 3 cups water.
Boil and simmer for 20 minutes until lentils are mushy.
Blend smooth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Add lemon juice and serve.
notes: Red lentils cook quickly and create a naturally creamy soup.

name: French Lentil Soup
preparation: Saute onion, garlic, carrot, and celery in olive oil.
Add lentils, thyme, bay leaf, and 3 cups water.
Simmer for 30-35 minutes until lentils are tender.
Remove thyme and bay leaf.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot with crusty bread.
notes: French lentils hold their shape well unlike red lentils.

name: Chicken Noodle Soup
preparation: Boil chicken in 4 cups water with bay leaf, strain broth.
Shred chicken and set aside.
Saute onion, garlic, carrot, and celery.
Add broth and bring to a boil.
Add noodles and cook until done.
Add shredded chicken, season, and serve.
notes: A comfort classic that helps fight colds.

name: Classic Chicken Soup
preparation: Boil chicken with bones in 4 cups water.
Skim froth and add onion, garlic, and bay leaf.
Simmer for 40 minutes.
Remove chicken, shred, strain broth.
Add carrot, celery, and chicken back to broth.
Cook for 10 minutes, season, and serve.
notes: Nothing beats a classic chicken soup for comfort.

name: Lemon Chicken Soup Greek
preparation: Cook chicken in stock, shred and set aside.
Cook orzo in the broth until tender.
Beat eggs with lemon juice.
Temper with a ladle of hot broth, stirring constantly.
Slowly pour back into the pot, stir gently.
Add chicken, dill, and season.
notes: Greek Avgolemono - never boil after adding the egg mixture.

name: Pho Bo Light
preparation: Char ginger and make broth with star anise and cinnamon.
Simmer meat in broth for 30 minutes.
Strain broth, slice meat thinly.
Cook rice noodles separately.
Place noodles and meat in bowl, pour hot broth.
Top with bean sprouts and herbs.
notes: The star anise and cinnamon are key to authentic pho flavor.

name: Pho Ga Light
preparation: Boil chicken with ginger and star anise.
Simmer for 25 minutes, strain broth.
Shred chicken.
Cook rice noodles separately.
Assemble noodles and chicken in bowl.
Pour hot broth, add lime, herbs, and sprouts.
notes: Pho Ga is the chicken version of Vietnamese pho.

name: Vietnamese Noodle Soup
preparation: Make broth with ginger, star anise, and chicken.
Simmer 25 minutes, strain and shred chicken.
Cook noodles separately.
Assemble noodles and chicken in bowls.
Pour hot broth over.
Serve with herbs, lime, and chilli.
notes: Customize with your choice of fresh herbs and condiments.

name: Wonton Soup Light
preparation: Bring stock to a boil with ginger and garlic.
Add wontons and cook for 5-6 minutes until they float.
Add bok choy and cook for 2 minutes.
Season with soy sauce and sesame oil.
Ladle into bowls.
Top with spring onions and serve.
notes: Wontons are done when they float to the surface.

name: Dumpling Soup
ingredients: Salt and white pepper to taste
preparation: Make dough by mixing flour, egg, and water. Knead until smooth, rest 30 minutes.
Mix ground meat with soy sauce, sesame oil, ginger, and green onions for filling.
Roll dough thin, cut into circles. Place filling in center, fold and pleat edges to seal.
Bring chicken broth to a boil in a large pot.
Add dumplings to boiling broth. Cook for 6-8 minutes until dumplings float and are cooked through.
Add bok choy in the last 2 minutes of cooking.
Season with rice vinegar, salt, and white pepper.
Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh cilantro.
notes: For vegetarian version, use mushroom and cabbage filling with vegetable broth.

name: Hot And Sour Soup Chinese
ingredients: 50g wood ear mushrooms, soaked and sliced
1/2 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 3 tbsp water
preparation: Bring broth to a boil in a large pot.
Add wood ear mushrooms and bamboo shoots. Simmer for 3 minutes.
Add tofu strips and cook for 2 minutes.
Stir in soy sauce, rice vinegar, and white pepper.
Slowly pour cornstarch slurry while stirring to thicken the soup.
Turn off heat and slowly drizzle beaten egg in a thin stream while stirring gently to create egg ribbons.
Add sesame oil and chili oil.
Garnish with sliced green onions and serve hot.
notes: The balance of vinegar (sour) and white pepper (hot) is key. Adjust to taste. Use vegetable broth for vegetarian version.

name: Butternut Squash Soup
ingredients: 1/6 large butternut squash (about 1kg), peeled and cubed
preparation: Heat butter in a large pot over medium heat.
Sauté onion and garlic until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add butternut squash and carrot. Cook for 3 minutes.
Pour in vegetable broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes until squash is very tender.
Blend soup until completely smooth using an immersion blender.
Stir in cream, nutmeg, and cinnamon. Season with salt and pepper.
Simmer for 5 more minutes on low heat.
Serve garnished with pumpkin seeds and crispy sage leaves.
notes: Roasting the squash before adding to soup intensifies the flavor. Can substitute cream with coconut milk for dairy-free version.

name: Roasted Pumpkin Soup
ingredients: 1/6 kg pumpkin, peeled and cut into chunks
preparation: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
Toss pumpkin chunks, onion quarters, and garlic with olive oil. Spread on a baking sheet.
Roast for 30-35 minutes until pumpkin is caramelized and tender.
Transfer roasted vegetables to a large pot. Add vegetable broth.
Bring to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes.
Blend until smooth using an immersion blender.
Stir in coconut cream, cumin, smoked paprika, and cayenne.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve topped with toasted pumpkin seeds and fresh thyme.
notes: Roasting the pumpkin brings out natural sweetness and depth of flavor that boiling cannot achieve.

name: Acorn Squash Soup
preparation: Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F). Place squash halves cut-side down on baking sheet. Roast for 40 minutes until tender.
Scoop out roasted squash flesh and set aside.
In a large pot, melt butter and sauté onion and garlic until softened.
Add apple and cook for 3 minutes.
Add squash flesh and vegetable broth. Simmer for 15 minutes.
Blend until smooth. Stir in cream, cinnamon, allspice, and nutmeg.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve with a swirl of sour cream.
notes: The apple adds a subtle sweetness that pairs beautifully with the nutty flavor of acorn squash.

name: Broccoli Cheddar Soup
preparation: Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat. Sauté onion and garlic until soft.
Sprinkle flour over onions and stir to make a roux. Cook for 2 minutes.
Gradually whisk in milk and broth until smooth.
Add broccoli florets and diced carrot. Simmer for 15-18 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Lightly mash or partially blend for a chunky texture (or fully blend for smooth).
Reduce heat to low. Gradually stir in shredded cheddar until melted and smooth.
Add nutmeg, salt, and pepper to taste.
Serve topped with extra shredded cheddar.
notes: Do not boil after adding cheese or it may become grainy. Low heat and gradual stirring ensures a smooth, creamy soup.

name: Cream Of Broccoli Soup
ingredients: 1 cup broccoli florets and stems, chopped
preparation: Melt butter in a large pot. Sauté onion and garlic until translucent.
Add potato and broccoli. Cook for 3 minutes, stirring.
Pour in vegetable broth. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 15-20 minutes until all vegetables are very soft.
Blend soup until completely smooth using an immersion blender.
Stir in heavy cream and nutmeg.
Season with salt and pepper. Simmer on low for 5 minutes.
Serve garnished with toasted almonds.
notes: The potato acts as a natural thickener, giving the soup body without excess flour or cream.

name: Broccoli Leek Soup
ingredients: 2/5 large leeks, white and light green parts, sliced
preparation: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Add sliced leeks and garlic, sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add potato and broccoli. Stir for 2 minutes.
Pour in vegetable broth. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 20 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Blend until smooth.
Stir in cream and lemon juice. Season with salt and pepper.
Simmer for 5 more minutes on low heat.
Serve garnished with fresh chives.
notes: Leeks add a mild, sweet onion flavor that complements broccoli beautifully. Wash leeks thoroughly to remove grit.

name: Corn Chowder
ingredients: 2/3 cups fresh or frozen corn kernels
preparation: Cook bacon in a large pot until crispy. Remove and set aside, leaving drippings.
Add butter, onion, and garlic to the pot. Sauté until softened.
Add diced potatoes and broth. Simmer for 12-15 minutes until potatoes are tender.
Add corn kernels and cook for 5 minutes.
Blend half the soup for a creamy base, return to pot.
Stir in heavy cream, milk, and thyme. Season with salt and pepper.
Simmer gently for 5 minutes. Do not boil.
Serve topped with crispy bacon bits and fresh chives.
notes: For vegetarian version, skip bacon and use olive oil and smoked paprika for smoky flavor.

name: New England Clam Chowder
ingredients: 150g fresh clams or 2 cans (90g) chopped clams with juice
1/4 tsp clam juice or fish stock
preparation: If using fresh clams, steam until opened. Remove meat, chop, and reserve liquid.
Cook bacon in a large pot until crispy. Remove bacon, keep drippings.
Add butter, onion, and celery. Sauté until soft, about 5 minutes.
Stir in flour and cook for 2 minutes to make a roux.
Gradually add clam juice, milk, bay leaf, and thyme. Stir until smooth.
Add potatoes and simmer for 15 minutes until tender.
Add chopped clams and heavy cream. Simmer for 5 minutes. Do not boil.
Remove bay leaf. Season with salt and pepper. Serve with oyster crackers and crispy bacon.
notes: New England style is cream-based (white), unlike Manhattan clam chowder which is tomato-based (red).

name: Seafood Chowder
ingredients: 200g firm white fish (cod or haddock), cubed
preparation: Cook bacon in a large pot until crispy. Remove and set aside.
Add butter, onion, and celery. Sauté until soft.
Sprinkle flour over vegetables and cook for 2 minutes, stirring.
Gradually stir in fish stock until smooth. Add bay leaf and potatoes.
Simmer for 12-15 minutes until potatoes are almost tender.
Add fish cubes and scallops. Cook for 4 minutes.
Add shrimp, cream, milk, and Old Bay seasoning. Simmer for 3-4 minutes until shrimp is pink.
Remove bay leaf. Season with salt and pepper. Serve topped with bacon and parsley.
notes: Add seafood in stages based on cooking time - fish first, then shrimp last to prevent overcooking.

name: Black Bean Soup
ingredients: 1/3 cups dried black beans, soaked overnight (or 3 cans drained)
Sour cream, cilantro, and lime wedges for garnish
preparation: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Sauté onion, garlic, jalapeño, celery, and carrot for 5 minutes.
Add cumin, smoked paprika, and oregano. Stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add soaked and drained black beans and vegetable broth.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 1-1.5 hours until beans are very tender (15 minutes if using canned).
Blend half the soup for a thick, creamy base. Return to pot.
Add lime juice and season with salt and pepper.
Serve topped with sour cream, fresh cilantro, and lime wedges.
notes: Black beans are an excellent source of plant protein and fiber. The smoky paprika gives depth without meat.

name: Tortilla Soup
ingredients: Crispy tortilla strips, avocado, sour cream, lime, and cilantro for topping
preparation: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Sauté onion, garlic, and jalapeño for 3 minutes.
Add cumin, chili powder, and smoked paprika. Stir for 30 seconds.
Add diced tomatoes, broth, and chicken breasts (whole).
Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 20 minutes until chicken is cooked.
Remove chicken, shred with two forks, and return to pot.
Add black beans and corn. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve topped with crispy tortilla strips, diced avocado, sour cream, lime juice, and cilantro.
notes: For crispy tortilla strips, cut corn tortillas into strips and bake at 190°C for 10 minutes until golden.

name: Pozole
ingredients: 3/8 dried guajillo chiles, stemmed and seeded
1/4 dried ancho chiles, stemmed and seeded
Shredded cabbage, radish slices, lime wedges, dried oregano, and tostadas for garnish
preparation: Place pork chunks in a large pot with water. Bring to a boil, skim foam. Add half the onion and 2 garlic cloves. Simmer for 1.5 hours until pork is tender.
Toast dried chiles in a dry pan for 1-2 minutes. Soak in hot water for 15 minutes.
Blend soaked chiles with remaining onion, garlic, oregano, cumin, and 1 cup soaking liquid until smooth.
Remove pork from broth, shred into pieces, return to pot.
Strain chile sauce into the pot. Add drained hominy.
Simmer for 30 minutes to meld flavors. Season with salt.
Serve in deep bowls with shredded cabbage, sliced radishes, lime wedges, dried oregano, and tostadas on the side.
notes: Pozole Rojo (red) uses dried red chiles. Pozole Verde uses tomatillos and green chiles. Both are traditional Mexican celebratory soups.

name: Ribollita
ingredients: 1/6 bunch lacinato kale (cavolo nero), tough stems removed, chopped
Extra virgin olive oil for drizzling
preparation: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Sauté onion, carrots, celery, and garlic for 5-7 minutes.
Add diced tomatoes and rosemary sprig. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add vegetable broth and Parmesan rind if using. Bring to a simmer.
Mash half the cannellini beans and add all beans to the pot.
Add chopped kale and simmer for 20 minutes until kale is tender.
Stir in torn stale bread pieces. The bread will absorb liquid and thicken the soup.
Let rest for 10 minutes (traditionally overnight and reboiled - hence 'ribollita' meaning 'reboiled').
Season with salt and pepper. Serve drizzled with extra virgin olive oil.
notes: A Tuscan peasant soup that tastes even better the next day when reheated. The bread makes it hearty and thick.

name: Tuscan White Bean Soup
preparation: Heat olive oil in a pot. Sauté onion, garlic, celery, and carrot for 5 minutes.
Add rosemary, sage, and red pepper flakes. Cook 1 minute.
Add beans and broth. Bring to a simmer.
Cook for 15 minutes. Remove herb sprigs.
Mash some beans against the side of the pot to create a creamy texture.
Add baby spinach and stir until wilted, about 2 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve with grated Parmesan and crusty bread.
notes: Simple, rustic Tuscan cooking at its finest. The key is quality olive oil and letting the beans shine.

name: Italian Wedding Soup
ingredients: For meatballs: 60g ground pork, 60g ground beef, 1/3 cup breadcrumbs, 1 egg, 2 tbsp Parmesan, 1 tsp garlic powder, salt and pepper
1/8 cup small pasta (acini di pepe or orzo)
preparation: Mix meatball ingredients. Roll into small balls (about 2cm). Set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large pot. Sauté onion, garlic, carrots, and celery for 5 minutes.
Add chicken broth and bring to a boil.
Carefully drop meatballs into boiling broth. Reduce to simmer and cook for 10 minutes.
Add pasta and cook until al dente, about 8-10 minutes.
Add chopped escarole or spinach. Cook for 3 minutes until wilted.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve with generous grated Parmesan.
notes: Despite the name, this soup has nothing to do with weddings. The Italian 'minestra maritata' means 'married soup' - the marriage of meat and greens.

name: Mushroom Bisque
ingredients: 1/10 cup dry sherry or white wine
preparation: Melt butter in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms in batches and cook until deeply browned, about 8 minutes total.
Add onion and garlic. Sauté for 3 minutes.
Sprinkle flour and stir for 2 minutes.
Deglaze with sherry or wine, scraping up browned bits.
Add broth and thyme sprig. Simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove thyme sprig. Blend until smooth.
Stir in cream and nutmeg. Season with salt and pepper.
Serve with a drizzle of truffle oil and fresh thyme.
notes: Browning mushrooms well is essential - it develops umami and deep flavor. Don't crowd the pan.

name: Cream Of Mushroom Soup
preparation: Melt butter in a pot. Sauté mushrooms until golden and liquid has evaporated, about 8 minutes.
Reserve a few mushroom slices for garnish.
Add onion and garlic. Cook for 3 minutes.
Stir in flour and cook for 2 minutes.
Gradually add broth and milk, stirring continuously to prevent lumps.
Add dried thyme. Simmer for 15 minutes.
Blend until smooth. Stir in heavy cream.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve garnished with reserved mushroom slices and parsley.
notes: Infinitely better than canned versions. Can be used as a base for casseroles and sauces too.

name: Wild Mushroom Soup
ingredients: 300g mixed wild mushrooms (porcini, chanterelle, morel), cleaned and sliced
100g dried porcini mushrooms, soaked in 1 cup hot water
preparation: Drain soaked porcini, reserving the liquid. Chop porcini roughly.
Heat butter and olive oil in a pot. Sauté fresh wild mushrooms until well browned, about 8 minutes.
Add shallot, garlic, and soaked porcini. Cook for 3 minutes.
Deglaze with Marsala wine.
Add broth, reserved porcini liquid (strain through fine cloth to remove grit), and rosemary.
Simmer for 20 minutes. Remove rosemary.
Blend half the soup for a partially chunky texture. Stir in cream.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve with crusty bread.
notes: Wild mushrooms have intense, earthy flavors. The dried porcini soaking liquid adds incredible depth - never discard it.

name: Potato Leek Soup
ingredients: 2/3 large leeks, white and light green parts, sliced
Salt and white pepper to taste
preparation: Thoroughly wash sliced leeks to remove all sand and grit.
Melt butter in a large pot. Sauté leeks and garlic over low heat for 10 minutes until very soft but not browned.
