Add potatoes, broth, bay leaf, and thyme. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes until potatoes are very tender.
Remove bay leaf. Blend until smooth.
Stir in cream. Season with salt and white pepper.
Serve garnished with chives and optional bacon bits.
notes: Cooking leeks slowly on low heat brings out their natural sweetness. This is the hot version of Vichyssoise.

name: Vichyssoise
ingredients: 2/3 large leeks, white parts only, sliced
Fresh chives, finely snipped, for garnish
preparation: Melt butter in a pot. Sauté leeks gently for 10 minutes until completely soft.
Add potatoes and broth. Simmer for 20-25 minutes until potatoes are very soft.
Blend until extremely smooth. Pass through a fine-mesh sieve for silky texture.
Stir in cream and milk. Season with salt and white pepper.
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight until thoroughly chilled.
Before serving, taste and adjust seasoning - cold soups need more salt.
Serve in chilled bowls, garnished with finely snipped chives.
notes: A classic French cold soup. Elegance lies in its silky smooth texture - straining is essential. Created by chef Louis Diat at the Ritz-Carlton New York.

name: Sweet Potato Soup
ingredients: 1/2 large sweet potatoes, peeled and cubed
Toasted coconut flakes and cilantro for garnish
preparation: Heat coconut oil in a pot. Sauté onion, garlic, and ginger for 3 minutes.
Add cumin, cinnamon, and cayenne. Stir for 30 seconds.
Add sweet potatoes and vegetable broth. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes until sweet potatoes are very tender.
Blend until smooth.
Stir in coconut milk. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve topped with toasted coconut flakes and fresh cilantro.
notes: Sweet potatoes and coconut milk create a naturally creamy, lightly sweet soup. The spices add warmth and complexity.

name: Roasted Tomato Basil Soup
preparation: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Place tomato halves cut-side up, onion, and garlic on a baking sheet.
Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Roast for 35-40 minutes until tomatoes are caramelized and slightly charred.
Transfer roasted vegetables to a pot. Add broth and fresh basil.
Simmer for 10 minutes. Blend until smooth.
Stir in cream if using and balsamic vinegar. Add sugar if needed.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve garnished with fresh basil. Perfect with grilled cheese.
notes: Roasting tomatoes concentrates their flavor and natural sweetness. This is miles ahead of any canned tomato soup.

name: Cream Of Tomato Soup
preparation: Melt butter in a pot. Sauté onion and garlic until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes to deepen the flavor.
Add canned tomatoes (with juices), crushing them with your hands as you add them.
Add broth, sugar, basil, and oregano. Simmer for 20 minutes.
Blend until completely smooth.
Return to pot and stir in heavy cream over low heat.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot with croutons.
notes: The sugar balances the acidity of tomatoes. Use San Marzano tomatoes for the best flavor.

name: Sun Dried Tomato Soup
preparation: Heat olive oil in a pot. Sauté onion and garlic until golden, about 5 minutes.
Add sun-dried tomatoes and diced tomatoes. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add vegetable broth and simmer for 15 minutes.
Blend until smooth.
Stir in cream, balsamic vinegar, and red pepper flakes.
Add fresh basil. Season with salt and pepper.
Serve with Parmesan crisps on top.
notes: Sun-dried tomatoes bring an intensely concentrated tomato flavor with sweet and slightly tangy notes.

name: Thai Coconut Soup Tom Kha
ingredients: 200g chicken breast, thinly sliced (or mushrooms for veg)
3/4 stalks lemongrass, bruised and cut into pieces
3/4 Thai bird's eye chillies, lightly crushed
preparation: Bring broth to a simmer. Add lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves. Simmer for 10 minutes to infuse.
Add coconut milk and bring back to a gentle simmer.
Add chicken slices and mushrooms. Cook for 5-7 minutes until chicken is done.
Add chillies, fish sauce, sugar, and lime juice. Stir gently.
Taste and adjust the balance of salty (fish sauce), sour (lime), and sweet (sugar).
Remove lemongrass and galangal pieces.
Serve in bowls garnished with fresh cilantro.
notes: Tom Kha differs from Tom Yum by being coconut milk-based (rich and creamy) vs clear broth (hot and sour).

name: Laksa
ingredients: 3/4 tbsp laksa paste (or blend: 4 dried chillies, 2 stalks lemongrass, 1 inch galangal, 4 shallots, 3 cloves garlic, 1 tsp belacan/shrimp paste, 1 tsp turmeric)
Bean sprouts, laksa leaves (Vietnamese mint), hard-boiled eggs, and sambal for garnish
preparation: Cook rice noodles according to package directions. Drain and set aside.
Heat oil in a pot. Fry laksa paste for 3-5 minutes until very fragrant and oil separates.
Add broth and coconut milk. Bring to a simmer.
Add tofu puffs and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add shrimp and cook for 3-4 minutes until pink.
Season with fish sauce and sugar.
Divide noodles among bowls. Ladle soup over noodles.
Top with bean sprouts, laksa leaves, halved hard-boiled eggs, and sambal on the side.
notes: Laksa is a spicy coconut curry noodle soup popular in Malaysia and Singapore. The paste is the key - homemade is best.

name: Malaysian Curry Soup
ingredients: Bean sprouts, fried shallots, lime wedges, and sambal for garnish
preparation: Heat oil in a pot. Fry shallots, garlic, ginger, and lemongrass until fragrant.
Add curry powder and cook for 2 minutes, stirring.
Add chicken pieces and sear for 3 minutes.
Pour in broth and coconut milk. Bring to a simmer and cook for 15 minutes.
Season with fish sauce and sugar.
Cook noodles separately according to package directions.
Divide noodles among bowls. Ladle curry soup over noodles.
Garnish with bean sprouts, fried shallots, lime wedges, and sambal.
notes: Malaysian curry is milder and more coconut-forward than Thai curry. The fried shallots add essential crunch and aroma.

name: Split Pea Soup
ingredients: 1/3 cups dried green split peas, rinsed
1/6 ham bone or 200g smoked ham hock
preparation: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Sauté onion, carrots, celery, and garlic for 5 minutes.
Add split peas, ham bone, water/broth, bay leaf, and thyme.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 60-75 minutes until peas are completely soft and falling apart.
Remove ham bone. Shred any meat from the bone and return meat to the pot.
Remove bay leaf. The soup will naturally thicken as it cools.
Season with salt and pepper. Add more water if too thick.
Serve with croutons.
notes: Split peas break down naturally without blending, creating a thick, hearty soup. For vegetarian, skip ham and add smoked paprika.

name: Green Pea Soup
ingredients: 4/5 cups fresh or frozen green peas
Mint leaves and cream drizzle for garnish
preparation: Melt butter in a pot. Sauté onion and garlic until softened.
Add potato and broth. Simmer for 15 minutes until potato is tender.
Add green peas and cook for 3-5 minutes (don't overcook or they lose their bright color).
Add fresh mint. Blend until very smooth.
Stir in cream and lemon juice. Season with salt and pepper.
For the brightest color, serve immediately or chill quickly.
Garnish with mint leaves and a cream drizzle.
notes: The key to vibrant green pea soup is not overcooking the peas. Add them last and blend quickly to preserve color.

name: Pea And Mint Soup
ingredients: Mint oil or fresh mint for garnish
preparation: Heat butter and olive oil in a pot. Sauté onion and garlic until soft.
Add broth and bring to a boil.
Add frozen peas directly. Cook for 3 minutes only.
Remove from heat. Add mint leaves immediately.
Blend until silky smooth.
Stir in Greek yogurt and lemon juice.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot or chilled, garnished with fresh mint.
notes: This soup is wonderful served cold in summer. The mint and lemon keep it fresh and bright.

name: Borscht
ingredients: 200g beef chuck, cubed (or skip for vegetarian)
Fresh dill and sour cream for serving
preparation: If using beef, simmer in broth for 1 hour until tender. Remove and shred.
Heat oil in a large pot. Sauté onion for 3 minutes.
Add grated beets and carrots. Cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes.
Add broth (with shredded beef if using), potatoes, bay leaf, and sugar.
Simmer for 15 minutes. Add shredded cabbage and cook for 10 more minutes.
Add garlic and red wine vinegar. Simmer 5 minutes. Remove bay leaf.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve with a generous dollop of sour cream and fresh dill.
notes: A Ukrainian/Eastern European staple. The vinegar preserves the beet's vibrant red color. Always serve with sour cream and dill.

name: Cabbage Soup
preparation: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Sauté onion, garlic, carrots, and celery for 5 minutes.
Add paprika and caraway seeds. Stir for 30 seconds.
Add shredded cabbage and cook for 5 minutes until slightly wilted.
Add diced tomatoes, broth, and bay leaf.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 25-30 minutes until cabbage is tender.
Add apple cider vinegar. Remove bay leaf.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve garnished with fresh parsley.
notes: A light, healthy soup packed with fiber. The caraway seeds add an Eastern European character. Very low calorie and filling.

name: Sauerkraut Soup
preparation: Melt butter in a pot. Brown sausage slices for 3 minutes. Remove and set aside.
Sauté onion, garlic, and carrot in the same pot for 5 minutes.
Add potatoes, broth, bay leaf, caraway seeds, and paprika. Simmer for 15 minutes.
Add drained sauerkraut and browned sausage. Simmer for 15 more minutes.
Remove bay leaf. Add some reserved sauerkraut juice to taste for extra tanginess.
Stir in sour cream until blended.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve garnished with fresh dill.
notes: A hearty Eastern European soup. The sauerkraut provides probiotics and a tangy flavor that pairs perfectly with smoked sausage.

name: Harira
ingredients: 0 cup vermicelli or broken angel hair pasta
Dates and chebakia for traditional serving
preparation: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Brown meat cubes on all sides.
Add onion and sauté for 3 minutes.
Add turmeric, ginger, cinnamon, pepper, and tomato paste. Stir for 2 minutes.
Add grated tomatoes, celery, lentils, and water. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 45 minutes until lentils and meat are tender.
Add chickpeas, vermicelli, cilantro, and parsley. Cook for 10 minutes.
Squeeze in lemon juice and season with salt.
Serve hot, traditionally with dates to break the fast during Ramadan.
notes: Morocco's national soup, traditionally served to break the fast during Ramadan. Rich, hearty, and deeply spiced.

name: Moroccan Chickpea Soup
preparation: Heat olive oil in a pot. Sauté onion and garlic until golden, about 5 minutes.
Add cumin, paprika, turmeric, cinnamon, and cayenne. Toast spices for 1 minute.
Add diced tomatoes and cook for 3 minutes.
Add chickpeas and vegetable broth. Bring to a simmer.
Cook for 20 minutes. Mash some chickpeas for a thicker consistency.
Add lemon juice and cilantro. Season with salt and pepper.
Serve with crusty bread.
notes: The warm North African spice blend with cinnamon is what makes this distinctly Moroccan. High in fiber and protein.

name: Moroccan Lentil Soup
preparation: Heat olive oil in a pot. Sauté onion, garlic, and ginger for 3 minutes.
Add cumin, paprika, turmeric, and cinnamon. Cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato paste and diced tomatoes. Cook for 3 minutes.
Add lentils and broth. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes until lentils are completely soft.
Blend until smooth (or leave chunky if preferred).
Stir in lemon juice and cilantro. Season with salt and pepper.
Serve with lemon wedges.
notes: Red lentils cook quickly and break down to a creamy consistency without blending. A staple across North Africa and the Middle East.

name: Egg Drop Soup Chinese
ingredients: 1 tsp cornstarch mixed with 2 tbsp cold water
preparation: Bring chicken broth to a boil in a pot.
Add soy sauce, white pepper, and ground ginger.
Stir in cornstarch slurry to slightly thicken the broth.
Reduce heat to a bare simmer.
Slowly pour beaten eggs in a thin stream while gently stirring the soup in one direction with a fork or chopstick.
The eggs should form silky ribbons. Stop stirring once all egg is added.
Remove from heat. Drizzle with sesame oil.
Serve immediately garnished with sliced green onions.
notes: The key to perfect egg ribbons is a thin pour into barely simmering (not boiling) broth. Ready in under 10 minutes.

name: Corn Egg Drop Soup
ingredients: 1/2 cups fresh or canned creamed corn
1/4 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 2 tbsp water
preparation: Bring chicken broth to a boil.
Add creamed corn and corn kernels. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add soy sauce and white pepper.
Stir in cornstarch slurry to thicken.
Reduce heat to low. Slowly drizzle beaten eggs while stirring gently.
Remove from heat. Add sesame oil.
Season with salt.
Serve garnished with sliced green onions.
notes: A Chinese-American restaurant favorite. The creamed corn gives it natural sweetness and body.

name: Spinach Egg Drop Soup
ingredients: 1/3 tsp cornstarch mixed with 1 tbsp water
preparation: Bring broth to a boil. Add garlic and cook 1 minute.
Add soy sauce and white pepper.
Stir in cornstarch slurry to lightly thicken.
Add fresh spinach and stir until just wilted, about 1 minute.
Reduce heat to a simmer. Slowly drizzle beaten eggs while stirring gently.
Remove from heat. Add sesame oil.
Season with salt.
Serve immediately garnished with green onion.
notes: A nutritious, quick soup. The spinach adds iron and vitamins while the egg provides protein. Use vegetable broth for vegetarian.

name: Clam Chowder Manhattan
ingredients: 150g fresh clams or 2 cans (90g) chopped clams with juice
preparation: Cook bacon in a large pot until crispy. Remove bacon, reserve drippings.
Sauté onion, celery, and carrots in drippings for 5 minutes.
Add tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add diced tomatoes, clam juice, water, potatoes, bay leaf, thyme, and oregano.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 20 minutes until potatoes are tender.
Add clams and cook for 5 minutes. Do not overcook.
Remove bay leaf. Season with salt and pepper.
Serve topped with crispy bacon and fresh parsley.
notes: Manhattan-style is tomato-based and lighter than New England's cream-based version. The rivalry between the two styles is legendary.

name: Lobster Bisque
ingredients: 1/2 whole lobsters (about 150g each) or 100g lobster meat and shells
Salt and white pepper to taste
Fresh chives and lobster meat for garnish
preparation: Cook lobsters in boiling water for 8 minutes. Cool, remove meat, and reserve shells.
Crush shells and roast in oven at 200°C for 15 minutes for deeper flavor.
Melt butter in a pot. Sauté onion, carrot, celery, and garlic for 5 minutes.
Add crushed roasted shells and stir for 3 minutes.
Carefully add brandy and flambé (or just cook off alcohol for 2 minutes).
Add tomato paste, stock, bay leaf, and thyme. Simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain through fine-mesh sieve, pressing shells to extract all flavor. Discard shells.
Return liquid to pot. Whisk in flour, then cream. Simmer until thickened.
Add cayenne, salt, and white pepper. Serve topped with chunks of lobster meat and chives.
notes: A bisque extracts maximum flavor from the shells. Roasting shells before simmering adds depth. This is fine dining in a bowl.

name: Shrimp Bisque
ingredients: 150g shrimp with shells (peel and reserve shells)
Salt and white pepper to taste
preparation: Peel shrimp and set aside. Roast shells in oven at 200°C for 10 minutes.
Melt butter in a pot. Sauté onion, carrot, celery, and garlic for 5 minutes.
Add roasted shrimp shells and cook for 3 minutes.
Add wine and cook for 2 minutes.
Stir in tomato paste, stock, and bay leaf. Simmer for 25 minutes.
Strain broth, discarding shells. Return to pot.
Whisk in flour and cream. Add paprika and cayenne.
Add peeled shrimp and cook for 3-4 minutes until pink.
Reserve some whole shrimp for garnish. Blend the rest until smooth.
Season with salt and white pepper. Serve garnished with whole shrimp and dill.
notes: Like lobster bisque, the secret is in the shells. They contain enormous flavor that enriches the broth exponentially.

name: Asparagus Soup
ingredients: 500g fresh asparagus, trimmed and cut into pieces (reserve tips)
Blanched asparagus tips and crème fraîche for garnish
preparation: Blanch asparagus tips in boiling water for 2 minutes. Reserve for garnish.
Melt butter in a pot. Sauté onion and garlic for 3 minutes.
Add asparagus pieces (stems and middle sections) and potato. Cook for 3 minutes.
Add broth. Simmer for 15-18 minutes until asparagus is very tender.
Blend until completely smooth. Pass through a sieve for silky texture.
Stir in cream and lemon juice. Season with salt and pepper.
Serve garnished with blanched asparagus tips and a dollop of crème fraîche.
notes: Use thick asparagus for soup - thin ones are better for grilling. The potato adds body without masking the delicate asparagus flavor.

name: Artichoke Soup
ingredients: 1 1/2 large artichoke hearts (fresh, frozen, or canned), quartered
Salt and white pepper to taste
preparation: Heat butter and olive oil in a pot. Sauté onion and garlic until soft.
Add artichoke hearts and potato. Cook for 3 minutes.
Add broth and thyme. Simmer for 20 minutes until everything is tender.
Remove thyme sprig. Blend until smooth.
Stir in cream and lemon juice. Season with salt and white pepper.
If desired, thinly slice an extra artichoke heart and fry in olive oil until crispy for garnish.
Serve with crispy artichoke chips on top.
notes: Artichokes have a subtle, nutty flavor. The lemon juice brightens the soup and prevents discoloration.

name: Fennel Soup
ingredients: 3/4 large fennel bulbs, trimmed and sliced (reserve fronds)
1 tbsp Pernod or pastis (optional, for anise flavor)
Salt and white pepper to taste
preparation: Heat butter and olive oil in a pot. Add sliced fennel and onion. Cook on medium-low for 15 minutes until fennel is very soft and slightly caramelized.
Add garlic and potato. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add broth and simmer for 20 minutes.
Blend until smooth.
Stir in cream, Pernod if using, and lemon juice.
Season with salt and white pepper.
Serve garnished with reserved fennel fronds.
notes: Cooking fennel slowly transforms its sharp anise flavor into a sweet, mellow taste. The Pernod is optional but adds sophistication.

name: Celery Soup
ingredients: 1/4 whole head of celery, chopped (leaves reserved)
Salt and white pepper to taste
Celery leaves and croutons for garnish
preparation: Melt butter in a pot. Sauté onion and garlic until soft.
Add chopped celery and potato. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add broth and celery seed. Simmer for 20-25 minutes until celery is very tender.
Blend until smooth.
Stir in cream. Season with salt and white pepper.
Serve garnished with reserved celery leaves and croutons.
notes: An underrated soup with a clean, fresh flavor. Celery is naturally low in calories and high in vitamins.

name: Cucumber Soup Cold
ingredients: 1 large cucumbers, peeled, seeded, and chopped
1/4 cup cold vegetable broth or cold water
Salt and white pepper to taste
Dill sprigs and olive oil for garnish
preparation: Reserve a small amount of cucumber for garnish.
Blend cucumbers, yogurt, sour cream, garlic, dill, mint, lemon juice, and cold broth until very smooth.
Season with salt and white pepper.
Add olive oil and blend briefly.
Refrigerate for at least 2 hours until well chilled.
Taste and adjust seasoning before serving (cold dulls flavors).
Serve in chilled bowls topped with reserved diced cucumber, dill sprigs, and a drizzle of olive oil.
notes: A refreshing summer soup similar to Greek tzatziki in soup form. Best served ice-cold on hot days.

name: Avocado Soup Cold
ingredients: Salt and white pepper to taste
Diced tomato, cilantro, tortilla chips, and lime wedges for garnish
preparation: Blend avocados, cold broth, buttermilk, lime juice, garlic, jalapeño (if using), cilantro, and cumin until perfectly smooth.
Season with salt and white pepper.
Refrigerate for at least 1 hour until well chilled.
Taste and adjust seasoning and lime juice before serving.
Serve in chilled bowls garnished with diced tomato, cilantro leaves, crushed tortilla chips, and lime wedges.
notes: Use perfectly ripe avocados for the creamiest texture. The lime juice prevents browning and adds freshness. Serve immediately after chilling as avocado can oxidize.
